reasoning: All four nail the core flaws (post hoc, confounded 2020, revenue-per-employee != productivity, headcount denominator, cherry-picked uncontrolled competitor comparison, false-dilemma jump to full RTO). D edges ahead by adding the falsifiability/hard-to-vary test (Flaw 6: the thesis would also fit a revenue rise) and the explicit 2x2 contingency table for the competitor claim, plus inflation-deflation and a natural-experiment design, the most structurally complete treatment. C is nearly identical and excellent (equivocation/semi-attached figure, premortem, inflation point) but slightly less novel than D. A is thorough and well-organized with a strong action plan but mislabels '12% over six years' implicitly and is a flat list with less framing discipline; B is solid and correctly computes the ~2.1% annualized figure but is the least structured and surfaces fewer non-obvious traps.
